LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS BEC/MCL LLC, JEFF KUENZLE AND VERONICA KUENZLE'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO DEFENDANTS BEC/MCL LLC AND JEFF KUENZLE AND GRANTED AS TO DEFENDANT VERONICA KUENZLE. THE FACTS PRESENTED BY BOTH THE MOVING AND OPPOSITION PAPERS SHOW THAT, PER MONCRIEF V. CLARK (2015) 238 CAL. APP. 4TH 1000, MOVING DEFENDANTS' INITIATION OF THE TRANSACTION AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE WITH A CALIFORNIA ENTITY IS SUFFICIENT TO WARRANT A CALIFORNIA COURT ASSERTING JURISDICTION OVER THE MOVING DEFENDANTS. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM AND THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: SHERRY L. SAWYER, CSR NO. 5976, EMAIL: SSAWYER@SFTC.ORG. (D302)
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY, (TRANSACTION ID # 100011051): PRO PER SUBSTITUTED FOR WEILL, ANDREW JOHN AS ATTORNEY FOR KUENZLE, JEFF

Notice Of Motion By Defendants Bec Mcl Llc Jeff Kuenzle And Veronica Kuenzle To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Oct-17-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Marc T. Cefalu of Cox Wooton Lerner Griffin & Hansen LLP (415.438.4600) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Andrew J. Weill of Weill & Mazer (415.421.0730) appears as counsel for defendants/moving parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court modifies and confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS BEC/MCL LLC, JEFF KUENZLE AND VERONICA KUENZLE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is DENIED as to Defendants BEC/MCL LLC and Jeff Kuenzle and GRANTED as to defendant Veronica Kuenzle. The facts presented by both the moving and opposition papers show that, per Moncrief v. Clark (2015) 238 Cal. App. 4th 1000, moving defendants' initiation of the transaction at issue in this case with a California entity is sufficient to warrant a California court asserting jurisdiction over the moving defendants. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Sherry L. Sawyer, CSR No. 5976, email: ssawyer@sftc.org. (D302)
